vision_description: The image shows an electrical drawing, specifically a wiring diagram for 3-phase starters, Bulletin 509 Sizes 7 and 8, with standard wiring and a start-stop push button station. The diagram is a detailed schematic of the electrical connections and components.
ack: 🔍 Reading your electrical print — a full interpretation takes ~30–60 s…
final_text:
📋 Allen-Bradley Bulletin 509 three-phase full-voltage magnetic motor starter (Sizes 7 and 8), wired for a standard START-STOP push-button station — catalog wiring page 17.

This page documents an across-the-line 3-phase motor starter. Incoming lines L1/L2/L3 pass through the main contactor poles, through current transformers (1/2/3 C.T.) and the three overload elements (O.L.'s), then out to the motor as T1/T2/T3. The lower elementary (ladder) diagram shows the control logic: a separate control source, protected by a 1-amp fuse, runs through a normally-closed Stop button and a normally-open Start button (sealed in by a CR relay contact) to energize the control relay CR; a normally-closed overload contact in series drops the circuit out on overload. Because Sizes 7 and 8 use very large contactors, the operating coil is DC-fed: a rectifier bridge converts the control AC to DC, an economizer circuit (1 & 2 Econ. Cap. plus 1 & 2 Res. switched by CR contacts) lowers the coil holding current after pull-in, and a MOV (Mov.) suppresses coil transients.

🔑 Signals
• Three-phase supply in
• Motor output leads
• Stop command (opens control circuit)
• Start command (energizes/seal-in via CR)
• Control-relay coil energize
• Overload trip (drops control circuit)
• Contactor coil (DC) hold

🔧 Devices
• Three-phase motor (MOTOR (T1/T2/T3))
• Main contactor operating coil (DC, economizer) (M / (L.B.))
• Line current transformers (1C.T. / 2C.T. / 3C.T.)
• Three-pole overload relay (O.L.'s)
• Control relay with seal-in and economizer contacts (CR (coil K1/K2; contacts A1X..A4X / A1Y..A4Y))
• Rectifier bridge (AC-to-DC for coil) (Rect.)
• Surge-suppression varistor (Mov.)
• Economizer capacitors (1 Econ. Cap. / 2 Econ. Cap.)
• Economizer resistors (1 Res. / 2 Res.)
• Control-circuit fuse (FU (1A))
• Stop push button (NC) (Stop (1-2))
• Start push button (NO) (Start (2-3))

🩺 If you're chasing a fault
… (more — reply "map")

❓ Couldn't confirm (verify on the sheet):
• Abbreviation '(L.B.)' at the M coil/contact is not clearly decodable.
• Exact AC/DC terminal assignment on the rectifier bridge (which of 4/8/9/12) is hard to read.
• Terminal '13' appears both at CR coil (K2) and near the OL/Grd area — possible dual usage needs a clean crop.
• FU block digit sequence '1 2 3 4 6 7' (whether '5' is intentionally skipped).
…and 1 more (see 'map')

⚠️ No voltage is printed on this sheet — neither the incoming line voltage nor the 'Separate Control Source' level is shown, so do not assume a value; treat the circuit as potentially lethal until measured. For continuity/resistance checks (Stop/Start contacts, coil, OL contact, capacitors, resistors) de-energize, lock out, and verify absence of voltage first. If live voltage checks are required to isolate coil vs OL, follow energized-work procedures with properly rated meters and PPE. Note that this is a generic catalog diagram and may not reflect the exact as-built panel.
🔎 Read from the drawing. Verify field conditions and use the correct procedure for the measurement.

Reply "map" for the full terminal and wire list.

map_text:
📐 Electrical print

🔌 Signals (exact — source → destination)
• Three-phase supply in — L1 / L2 / L3  @ poles 20/22/24  → main contactor poles then motor · conf 0.90
• Motor output leads — T1 / T2 / T3  @ T1/T2/T3  → MOTOR · conf 0.90
• Stop command (opens control circuit) — Stop  @ 1-2  → control-relay CR rung · conf 0.90
• Start command (energizes/seal-in via CR) — Start  @ 2-3  → CR coil (K1)-(K2) · conf 0.90
• Control-relay coil energize — CR (K1/K2)  @ 3 to 13  → operates contactor coil economizer circuit · conf 0.85
• Overload trip (drops control circuit) — O.L.  @ 13 to 4  → opens series with CR coil · conf 0.85
• Contactor coil (DC) hold — M / (L.B.)  @ 6-7-8-9  → pulls in main contactor via rectifier + economizer · conf 0.60

🔧 Devices (16)
• MOTOR — three-phase induction motor (Motor load with leads T1, T2, T3 (shown both at MOTOR box lower-left and at contactor output T1/T2/T3)) · conf 0.95
• M — main contactor coil (DC, economizer-operated) (Contactor operating coil shown as (M) with '(L.B.)' near terminals 6, 7, 8, 9; on Sizes 7/8 the coil is DC-fed through a rectifier) · conf 0.60
• 1C.T. — current transformer (Line current transformer in phase feeding T1) · conf 0.90
• 2C.T. — current transformer (Line current transformer in phase feeding T2) · conf 0.90
• 3C.T. — current transformer (Line current transformer in phase feeding T3) · conf 0.90
• O.L.'s — overload relay assembly (3-pole) (Three overload heater/sensing elements with terminals 20/22/24 top and 21/23/25 bottom; drives a control-circuit overload contact) · conf 0.90
• CR — control relay (coil K1-K2, contacts A1X..A4X / A1Y..A4Y) (Master control relay; coil marked between K1 (3) and K2 (13); provides seal-in and economizer switching contacts) · conf 0.85
• Rect. — rectifier bridge (AC-to-DC bridge feeding the DC contactor coil; corners marked + and -, terminals include 8, 9, 12, 4) · conf 0.75
• Mov. — metal-oxide varistor (surge suppressor) (Transient suppressor across the coil circuit near the rectifier, terminals 4, 8, 12) · conf 0.80
• 1 Econ. Cap. — economizer capacitor (Energy-saving (economizer) capacitor for coil holding circuit, terminals 8 and 5) · conf 0.85
• 2 Econ. Cap. — economizer capacitor (Second economizer capacitor for coil holding circuit, terminals 8 and 5) · conf 0.85
• 1 Res. — resistor (Economizer/discharge resistor in coil circuit (rung with CR A2/A3 contacts)) · conf 0.80
• 2 Res. — resistor (Economizer/holding resistor in coil circuit (rung with CR A4 contact)) · conf 0.80
• FU — control-circuit fuse (1A) (Control fuse '1A' in the separate control source line; also a fuse/terminal block marked FU with 1 2 3 4 6 7) · conf 0.85
…and 2 more

🧩 1 PE bond(s)

⚠️ Couldn't read (retake or crop closer):
• Abbreviation '(L.B.)' at the M coil/contact is not clearly decodable.
• Exact AC/DC terminal assignment on the rectifier bridge (which of 4/8/9/12) is hard to read.
• Terminal '13' appears both at CR coil (K2) and near the OL/Grd area — possible dual usage needs a clean crop.
• FU block digit sequence '1 2 3 4 6 7' (whether '5' is intentionally skipped).
• No system voltages are printed anywhere on the sheet.

🔎 Proposed interpretation from the drawing — not yet field-verified. Reply to confirm or correct any item.

package.subtitle: Bulletin 509 Sizes 7 and 8 — Standard wiring with START-STOP push button station
package.note: Generic catalog wiring diagram; designations are AB/reference-style, not DIN/IEC 81346 project tags.

Contactor operating coil shown as (M) with '(L.B.)' near terminals 6, 7, 8, 9; on Sizes 7/8 the coil is DC-fed through a rectifier
Line current transformer in phase feeding T1
Line current transformer in phase feeding T2
Line current transformer in phase feeding T3
Three overload heater/sensing elements with terminals 20/22/24 top and 21/23/25 bottom; drives a control-circuit overload contact

devices.type: control relay (coil K1-K2, contacts A1X..A4X / A1Y..A4Y)
devices.detail: Master control relay; coil marked between K1 (3) and K2 (13); provides seal-in and economizer switching contacts
devices.evidence: 'CR', coil '3 -O- 13' with K1/K2, contact array A1X-A4X / A1Y-A4Y

devices.detail: AC-to-DC bridge feeding the DC contactor coil; corners marked + and -, terminals include 8, 9, 12, 4
Transient suppressor across the coil circuit near the rectifier, terminals 4, 8, 12
Energy-saving (economizer) capacitor for coil holding circuit, terminals 8 and 5
Second economizer capacitor for coil holding circuit, terminals 8 and 5
Economizer/discharge resistor in coil circuit (rung with CR A2/A3 contacts)
devices.evidence: '1 Res.' resistor box, near terminals 5/7 and RES block '5 1 7'

devices.detail: Economizer/holding resistor in coil circuit (rung with CR A4 contact)
devices.evidence: '2 Res.' resistor box, near terminals 12/6 and RES block '12 2 6'

devices.detail: Control fuse '1A' in the separate control source line; also a fuse/terminal block marked FU with 1 2 3 4 6 7
devices.evidence: 'FU 1A' fuse symbol at 'Separate Control Source'; 'FU' block '1 2 3 4 6 7' with '1A'

devices.detail: Normally-open start pushbutton, terminals 2 and 3, sealed by CR contact

terminals.evidence: '4' at Separate Control Source return and rectifier AC
'5' at Econ. Cap. and RES block
'8' at coil, rectifier and Econ. Caps
'13' at CR coil (K2) and near OL/Grd

contacts.detail: Normally-open CR contact in parallel with Start push button (seal/hold-in)
CR contact in coil/economizer rung, terminal 5
CR contact in coil/economizer rung, terminal 7
CR contact switching economizer resistor 2 Res. in coil circuit, terminals 12/6
Normally-closed overload contact in series with control relay coil; opens on overload to drop out coil
Contactor-driven contact ('L.B.') in economizer coil rung, near terminal 8

power_domains.detail: Incoming Lines L1, L2, L3 to contactor main poles; voltage not printed
Control circuit fed from a separate source through fuse 1A; voltage not printed
power_domains.evidence: 'Separate Control Source' with FU 1A and terminal 4

pe_bonds.detail: Ground connection shown at the overload/coil-circuit area
pe_bonds.evidence: 'Grd.' with ground symbol near O.L.'s box

unresolved.resolution: Confirm against Bulletin 509 legend; likely a coil/contact designation but not decodable from image alone

unresolved.item: Exact AC/DC corner assignments of the rectifier bridge (which of 4/8/9/12 are AC vs DC)
unresolved.resolution: High-resolution crop of the 'Rect.' bridge region

unresolved.item: Terminal '13' appears at both CR coil (K2) and near the O.L./Grd. area
unresolved.resolution: Crop of OL box '4 [symbol] 13 Grd.' to confirm

unresolved.item: FU block digits '1 2 3 4 6 7' and whether '5' is intentionally omitted

graph.cross_sheet_notes: This is a self-contained catalog page (AB Bulletin 509, page 17/13). It shows both a connection/wiring layout (upper) and an elementary/ladder diagram (lower) for the SAME Size 7/8 starter; no genuine off-page continuation references are present.

brief.sheet_title: Allen-Bradley Bulletin 509 three-phase full-voltage magnetic motor starter (Sizes 7 and 8), wired for a standard START-STOP push-button station — catalog wiring page 17.
brief.purpose: This page documents an across-the-line 3-phase motor starter. Incoming lines L1/L2/L3 pass through the main contactor poles, through current transformers (1/2/3 C.T.) and the three overload elements (O.L.'s), then out to the motor as T1/T2/T3. The lower elementary (ladder) diagram shows the control logic: a separate control source, protected by a 1-amp fuse, runs through a normally-closed Stop button and a normally-open Start button (sealed in by a CR relay contact) to energize the control relay CR; a normally-closed overload contact in series drops the circuit out on overload. Because Sizes 7 and 8 use very large contactors, the operating coil is DC-fed: a rectifier bridge converts the control AC to DC, an economizer circuit (1 & 2 Econ. Cap. plus 1 & 2 Res. switched by CR contacts) lowers the coil holding current after pull-in, and a MOV (Mov.) suppresses coil transients.

key_signals.destination: pulls in main contactor via rectifier + economizer

key_devices.device: Control relay with seal-in and economizer contacts

brief.troubleshooting_example: Symptom: pressing Start does not pull in the contactor. First verify the control string with power on and rated PPE: check for voltage/continuity through Stop (terminals 1-2) and that Start makes across 2-3. If 1-2-3 is good but CR never energizes, you must separate a bad relay coil from an open overload path: measure across the CR coil (K1)-(K2) leads (terminals 3 and 13) — if there is voltage across it but it doesn't operate, the coil is the fault; if there is NO voltage there, move to the overload contact and measure from terminal 13 through the O.L. contact to terminal 4 — an open there (tripped/failed OL contact) is the cause, not the relay. NOTE: this drawing shows the control logic but does not prove the actual field wiring condition.
brief.safety_context: No voltage is printed on this sheet — neither the incoming line voltage nor the 'Separate Control Source' level is shown, so do not assume a value; treat the circuit as potentially lethal until measured. For continuity/resistance checks (Stop/Start contacts, coil, OL contact, capacitors, resistors) de-energize, lock out, and verify absence of voltage first. If live voltage checks are required to isolate coil vs OL, follow energized-work procedures with properly rated meters and PPE. Note that this is a generic catalog diagram and may not reflect the exact as-built panel.
unresolved_items: Abbreviation '(L.B.)' at the M coil/contact is not clearly decodable.
Exact AC/DC terminal assignment on the rectifier bridge (which of 4/8/9/12) is hard to read.
Terminal '13' appears both at CR coil (K2) and near the OL/Grd area — possible dual usage needs a clean crop.
FU block digit sequence '1 2 3 4 6 7' (whether '5' is intentionally skipped).
No system voltages are printed anywhere on the sheet.
brief.purpose_note: Inference: the seal-in/economizer function is standard AB 509 behavior; only what is labeled on the sheet is asserted as fact.
